message:
feat(http): implement proper HEAD request handling per RFC 7231

Add HTTP HEAD method support that correctly strips response bodies while
preserving headers as required by RFC 7231 Section 4.3.2. HEAD responses
must return the same headers as GET (including Content-Length) but with
an empty message body.

Implementation details:
- Add strip_body_for_head() function that decompose/rebuilds responses
- Preserve existing Content-Length header if already set
- Compute and add Content-Length for known-length bodies (Bytes, Empty)
- Do not add Content-Length for streaming bodies (length unknown)
- Strip body while keeping all other headers intact

Test coverage:
- head_request_returns_empty_body: verify body is stripped
- head_request_preserves_content_type: verify headers preserved
- head_request_adds_content_length_for_known_body: verify Content-Length

Also includes Cargo.lock update and beads issue tracker sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -139,7 +139,7 @@
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-1kw","title":"HTTP 100-continue handling for large uploads","description":"Handle Expect: 100-continue header for large POST/PUT requests. Server should validate auth/content-type before telling client to send body, saving bandwidth on rejected requests.

**Implementation:**
- Check Expect header during request line parsing
- If 100-continue present, pause before body read
- Call pre-body validation hooks (auth, content-type check)
- Send '100 Continue' response if validation passes
- Send 417 Expectation Failed if validation fails
- Then proceed to read body

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Expect: 100-continue handled correctly
- Pre-body validation hooks called before 100 response
- 417 sent when validation fails
- Normal requests without Expect work unchanged
- Integration with auth middleware

**Tests:**
- POST with Expect gets 100 then proceeds
- POST with Expect and bad auth gets 401, no body read
- POST without Expect works normally
- Large upload with 100-continue completes successfully","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:34.843987822Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:34.843987822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","phase-1","uploads"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-1kw","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-1lu","title":"HTTPDigest auth extractor (stub)","description":"HTTP Digest authentication stub (FastAPI only provides stub). Extract Authorization: Digest header, return HTTPAuthorizationCredentials with scheme=Digest and credentials. Does NOT implement full Digest challenge-response. OpenAPI security scheme type: http with scheme=digest. auto_error behavior same as other auth extractors.  {"id":"fastapi_rust-1o5","title":"OpenAPI generation test suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for OpenAPI generation:

TEST CATEGORIES:

1. PATH GENERATION
- Static paths
- Path parameters
- Query parameters
- Header parameters
- Multiple parameters

2. SCHEMA GENERATION
- Primitive types
- Structs with all field types
- Nested structs
- Enums
- Optional/nullable
- Arrays and maps

3. SCHEMA DEDUPLICATION
- Same type used twice = one schema
- Nested type refs
- Circular reference handling

4. REQUEST BODY
- JSON body schema
- Form body schema
- File upload schema
- Multiple content types

5. RESPONSES
- Default response
- Multiple status codes
- Response headers
- Error responses

6. SECURITY
- Bearer auth scheme
- Basic auth scheme
- API key schemes
- OAuth2 flows
- Scopes documentation

7. VALIDATION
- Generated doc is valid OpenAPI 3.1
- Validates against official schema
- Swagger UI can render it

COVERAGE TARGET: 95%+ for OpenAPI code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:16:10.673742988Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:20:50.811649896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:20:50.811586648Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive OpenAPI generation test suite: 25 new integration tests covering path generation (7 tests), schema deduplication (4 tests), request body handling (4 tests), responses (1 test), metadata (4 tests), serialization (4 tests), plus full integration test.  {"id":"fastapi_rust-e5i","title":"Request/response logging middleware","description":"Implement middleware for automatic request/response logging:
- Log request: method, path, headers (filtered), body size

- Sensitive headers redacted by default
- Duration accurate to microsecond
- Body truncation works correctly

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-fac","title":"Connection draining on shutdown","description":"Implement graceful connection draining:
- Stop accepting new connections on shutdown signal
- Wait for in-flight requests to complete (with timeout)
- Force-close connections after drain timeout
- Integration with asupersync region cancellation

DEPENDS ON: Graceful shutdown, TCP server

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- In-flight requests complete during shutdown
- New connections rejected during drain
- Drain timeout forces closure
- Clean exit with no resource leaks

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Integration: Shutdown during active requests
- Integration: Drain timeout behavior
- Unit: Signal handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:45.332377934Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:38.952857482Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:38.952790225Z","close_reason":"Implemented connection draining on shutdown:
- Added drain_timeout config (default 30s)
- Added draining AtomicBool flag to TcpServer
- Added is_draining(), start_drain(), wait_for_drain(), drain() methods
- Accept loop checks draining flag and returns ServerError::Shutdown
- wait_for_drain polls connection count with configurable interval
- drain() combines start_drain + wait_for_drain with configured timeout
- Added 11 unit tests for drain functionality
- Total test count: 210 passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["drain","phase-1","server","shutdown"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-fac","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-n12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-ieq","title":"RequestValidationError with location tuples","description":"Implement validation error aggregation:

STRUCTURE:
- errors: Vec<ValidationErrorItem>
- body: Option<serde_json::Value> (the invalid input)

ValidationErrorItem:
- type_: String (error type: 'missing', 'string_too_short', etc)
- loc: Vec<LocItem> (location: ['body', 'field', 'nested'])
- msg: String (human-readable message)
- input: serde_json::Value (the invalid value)
- ctx: Option<HashMap> (constraint context)

LocItem enum:
- String(String) for field names
- Int(usize) for array indices

LOCATION PREFIXES (per FastAPI):
- Path params: ['path', 'param_name']
- Query params: ['query', 'param_name']
- Headers: ['header', 'header_name']
- Cookies: ['cookie', 'cookie_name']
- Body: ['body'] or ['body', 'field', 'nested']

RESPONSE FORMAT (422):
{
  "detail": [
    {"type": "missing", "loc": ["query", "q"], "msg": "Field required", "input": null}
  ]
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Errors collected from all extractors
- Proper location tuples
- FastAPI-compatible JSON format
- Multiple errors aggregated

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each error type formatting
- Unit: Nested location paths
- Unit: Array index locations
- Integration: Multiple validation errors
- Compatibility: Match FastAPI format exactly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:09:50.010991892Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.348797686Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T16:27:26.167074955Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","http","phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-ieq","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-zom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-ikb","title":"HTTP parser security hardening","description":"Security hardening for HTTP parser:
- Request line length limits
- Header count limits (max headers)
- Individual header size limits  
- Total header block size limits
- Request smuggling prevention (strict parsing)
- Invalid byte rejection in headers

DEPENDS ON: Request line parser, Header parser

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All limits configurable
- Clear error messages for violations
- No ambiguous parsing (strict mode)
- Reject null bytes and other invalid chars

SECURITY:
- CVE: HTTP request smuggling prevention
- CVE: Header injection prevention
- DOS: Resource exhaustion protection

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each limit enforcement
- Security: Known smuggling payloads rejected
- Security: Malformed header injection attempts
- Fuzz: Random input handling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:17.833440814Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.356069852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T15:43:51.688890235Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","parser","phase-1","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-ikb","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-iq2","title":"Snapshot testing for response bodies","description":"Support snapshot testing for API responses. Automatically detect unexpected response changes.

Implementation:
- Capture and store response snapshots
- Compare responses against snapshots
- Update snapshots with flag
- Ignore dynamic fields (timestamps, IDs)
- JSON and text snapshot support

Features:
- assert_snapshot!(response) macro
- Inline vs file snapshots
- Field masking for dynamic values
- Diff output on mismatch
- CI-friendly (fail on new snapshots)

Acceptance Criteria:
- Snapshots stored in tests directory
- Comparison detects changes
- Update mode refreshes snapshots
- Dynamic field masking works
- Clear diff output

Tests:
- Matching snapshot passes
- Changed response fails
- Update mode creates new snapshot
- Masked fields ignored in comparison","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:16:16.512633141Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:16:16.512633141Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["regression","snapshots","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-iq2","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-j3v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-j3v","title":"Assertion helpers for testing","description":"Testing assertion helpers:
- assert_status!(response, 200)
- assert_json!(response, json!({...}))
- assert_header!(response, 'Content-Type', 'application/json')
- assert_body_contains!(response, 'text')
- Detailed error messages on failure

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Assertions work with TestClient responses
- Clear failure messages
- Partial JSON matching
- Regex support for strings

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-jtb","title":"Implement derive macro for Validate trait","description":"Create proc macro for validation derive:

ATTRIBUTE SYNTAX:
#[derive(Validate)]
struct CreateUser {
    #[validate(length(min = 1, max = 100))]
    name: String,
    
    #[validate(email)]
    email: String,
    
    #[validate(range(ge = 0, le = 150))]
    age: i32,
    
    #[validate(regex = r'^[a-z]+$')]
    username: String,
    
    #[validate(custom = validate_password)]
    password: String,
    
    #[validate(nested)]
    address: Address,
}

SUPPORTED VALIDATORS:
- length(min, max) - String/Vec length
- range(gt, ge, lt, le) - Numeric bounds
- email - Email format
- url - URL format
- regex = 'pattern' - Regex match
- custom = function - Custom validator
- nested - Validate nested struct
- multiple_of = N - Divisibility check

ERROR GENERATION:
Each validator generates ValidationErrorItem with:
- type_: validator name
- loc: field path
- msg: human-readable message
- ctx: constraint values

GENERATED CODE:
impl Validate for CreateUser {
    fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ValidationErrors> {
        let mut errors = vec![];
        if self.name.len() < 1 { errors.push(...); }
        if self.name.len() > 100 { errors.push(...); }
        // ... more validations
        if errors.is_empty() { Ok(()) } else { Err(ValidationErrors(errors)) }
    }
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All validators generate correct code
- Error messages are clear
- Nested validation works
- Custom validators supported
- Compile-time field type checking

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each validator type
- Unit: Multiple validators per field
- Unit: Nested struct validation
- Unit: Custom validator integration
- Unit: Error message quality
- Compile-fail: Invalid attribute usage","acceptance_criteria":"derive(Validate) generates working validators with descriptive errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-01-17T14:20:06.943263912Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:44:02.308239143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:44:02.308174102Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented in crates/fastapi-macros/src/validate.rs: #[derive(Validate)] with all validators: length(min, max), range(gt, ge, lt, le), email, url, regex, custom, nested, and multiple_of. Full code generation with proper error types and field location tracking.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-3","proc-macro","validation"]}
